allies.rationale_text: Cognitive-load-theory helps generation-effect work by chunking complex material so active articulation builds real structure instead of overload.
allies.source_quote: By forcing information into small, digestible chunks or steps, generation aligns with reducing cognitive load.

allies.rationale_text: Feynman-technique operationalizes generation-effect by forcing plain-English explanation and analogy that reveal whether the idea is truly understood.

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias weakens generation-effect when self-produced explanations are treated as proof instead of being checked against evidence and outside correction.
antagonists.source_quote: Danger when "generate your own answer" becomes an excuse to skip expert correction, evidence checks, or external calibration.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Generation-effect conflicts with simplification when requiring learners to generate answers adds friction that direct instruction would avoid in time-critical contexts.
structured_tensions.source_quote: generation-effect conflicts with simplification (simplification) when requiring learners to generate answers adds friction that direct instruction would avoid in time-critical contexts.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Generation-effect conflicts with cognitive-load-theory when generation demands exceed working memory capacity, causing the effortful process to degrade rather than enhance learning.
structured_tensions.source_quote: generation-effect conflicts with cognitive-load-theory (cognitive load theory) when generation demands exceed working memory capacity, causing the effortful process to degrade rather than enhance learning.

curation_notes.summary: The source is strongest on active explanation, chunked understanding, and analogy testing, so the first-order relation surface stays compact.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broader presentation-design relation sprawl because the cleanest current-vocabulary links are cognitive-load-theory and feynman-technique.
open_questions: The confirmation-bias antagonist is source-backed as a calibration risk but not presented as a direct explicit tension block.
